claim: Organize every codebase with three essential directories: ai-docs (persistent API docs), specs (feature plans), and .claude (reusable commands) to unlock agentic coding at scale
actionable: Create these three directories in all your projects; populate ai-docs with third-party API docs; write detailed feature specs; set up reusable context-priming commands
source_title: Agentic Claude Code: 3 Codebase Folders for TOP 1% AI Coding

claim: The context-priming command is the single most important reusable prompt—it reads your README, lists project files, and gives AI agents instant understanding of project structure on each new session
actionable: Create a /context-prime command that runs get tree (or ls), reads README, and outputs a project summary; use it at the start of every session to avoid context window waste
source_title: Agentic Claude Code: 3 Codebase Folders for TOP 1% AI Coding

claim: Detailed feature specs in your specs/ directory act as the prompt—great planning IS great prompting, and agentic tools can execute a well-written spec with self-validation without iterative back-and-forth
actionable: Write specs with problem statement, solution, file changes, test updates, and self-validation sections; avoid iterative prompting; hand off the spec and let the agent execute
source_title: Agentic Claude Code: 3 Codebase Folders for TOP 1% AI Coding

claim: Use 'plan drafting' where the AI writes the first draft of a spec by reading your codebase and README, then you iterate on the plan before execution—not during implementation
actionable: Ask the agent to draft a spec given the context prime; review and edit the spec; then run the spec once with agentic mode enabled
source_title: Agentic Claude Code: 3 Codebase Folders for TOP 1% AI Coding

claim: AI agents should only be given critical context—too much context confuses agents as much as too little does; use feature-specific context priming for large changes to avoid token waste
actionable: When working on a large change, create a feature-specific context-prime variant that reads only relevant files; don't give agents access to your entire codebase
source_title: Agentic Claude Code: 3 Codebase Folders for TOP 1% AI Coding

claim: Agentic coding enables the full SDLC in a single prompt—planning, coding, testing, and self-validation—whereas AI coding only generates code snippets for iterative review
actionable: Shift from iterative prompting (AI coding) to spec-first planning + full execution (agentic coding); require self-validation in every spec
source_title: Agentic Claude Code: 3 Codebase Folders for TOP 1% AI Coding
